meanings.usage: 指研究恒星、星系等天体的科学
表示通过仪器或方法发现或感知某物的存在
指由众多恒星、气体和尘埃组成的巨大天体系统
指生物或事物经过漫长时间逐渐发展变化
常与of连用，表示有能力做某事
指天体运行的路径，或绕某天体运行的动作
指包围地球的气体层，或某地的一般氛围
指产生某种物质、能量或结果
指思想、活动或知识的领域或范围
blot out表示遮住、挡住光线
正式用语，指尝试做某事或努力做的事

examples.en: In relative terms, planets are very small and do not emit light.
examples.zh: 相对而言，行星非常小且不发光。

examples.en: A planet would have to be a very long way from it to be capable of supporting life.
examples.zh: 行星必须离它很远才能维持生命。

examples.en: In the first instance, we would be looking for plant life, rather than 'little green men'.
examples.zh: 首先，我们会寻找植物生命，而不是小绿人。

examples.en: But this hope is always in the realms of science fiction.
examples.zh: 但这种希望总是在科幻小说的领域里。

examples.en: We would have to find a way of blotting out the light from its star.
examples.zh: 我们必须找到一种方法遮住它恒星的光。

examples.en: In the first instance, we would be looking for plant life.
examples.zh: 首先，我们会寻找植物生命。

examples.en: Venus is far too hot and Mars is far too cold to support life.
examples.zh: 金星太热而火星太冷，都无法维持生命。

phrases.phrase: take + 时间 + for + 事物 + to do

examples.en: It has taken more than four billion years for plant and animal life to evolve.
examples.zh: 动植物生命进化花了四十多亿年。

examples.en: It is impossible to look for life on another planet using earth-based telescopes.
examples.zh: 用地面望远镜寻找另一颗行星上的生命是不可能的。

examples.en: It is bacteria that have generated the oxygen we breathe on earth.
examples.zh: 正是细菌产生了我们呼吸的氧气。

grammar.title: 虚拟语气 (Subjunctive Mood) - 与现在事实相反
grammar.definition: 虚拟语气用于表达与事实相反的假设情况。使用were来表示与现在事实相反的假设
grammar.structure: If + 主语 + were + 其他, 主语 + would/could/might + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 用于表达不太可能实现的假设或与事实相反的情况

examples.en: If we were able to discover lowly forms of life like bacteria on another planet, it would completely change our view of ourselves.
examples.zh: 如果我們能夠在另一颗行星上发现像细菌这样的低等生命，那将完全改变我们对自己的看法。

grammar.title: 定语从句 (Relative Clause) - 关系副词where引导
grammar.definition: 用where引导的定语从句修饰表示地点的名词，说明事件发生的场所
grammar.structure: 名词 + where + 完整句子
grammar.usage: where在从句中作地点状语，修饰其前的名词

examples.en: A telescope would have to be as far away as the planet Jupiter to look for life in outer space, where the dust becomes thinner.
examples.zh: 望远镜必须远到像木星那样的距离才能在外太空寻找生命，那里的尘埃更稀薄。

grammar.title: 强调句型 (Emphatic Structure) - It is/was + 被强调部分 + that/who + 其他
grammar.definition: 使用It is/was...that/who...结构强调句子的某个成分
grammar.structure: It is/was + 强调部分 + that/who + 句子其他部分
grammar.usage: 可用于强调主语、宾语或状语，增强表达效果

examples.en: It is bacteria that have generated the oxygen we breathe on earth.
examples.zh: 正是细菌产生了我们呼吸的氧气。

original.en: Whether a planet can support life depends on the size and brightness of its star.
original.zh: 行星是否能维持生命取决于其恒星的大小和亮度。

imitations.en: Whether we can find water on Mars depends on its geological conditions.
imitations.zh: 我们能否在火星上找到水取决于它的地质条件。

imitations.en: Whether the plan will succeed depends on everyone's cooperation.
imitations.zh: 这个计划是否成功取决于每个人的配合。

sentencePatterns.pattern: The first question to answer is whether...

original.en: The first question to answer is whether a planet can actually support life.
original.zh: 首先要回答的问题是行星是否真的能维持生命。

imitations.en: The first problem to solve is whether we have enough funding.
imitations.zh: 首先要解决的问题是我们是否有足够的资金。

imitations.en: The first step to take is whether we should accept their offer.
imitations.zh: 首先要采取的步骤是我们是否应该接受他们的提议。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is + adj + to do something

original.en: It is impossible to look for life on another planet using earth-based telescopes.
original.zh: 用地面望远镜寻找另一颗行星上的生命是不可能的。

imitations.en: It is difficult to detect planets in distant galaxies.
imitations.zh: 探测遥远星系中的行星是困难的。

imitations.en: It is necessary to understand the basic principles of astronomy.
imitations.zh: 理解天文学的基本原理是必要的。

sentencePatterns.pattern: A planet would have to be...to be capable of...

original.en: A planet would have to be a very long way from it to be capable of supporting life.
original.zh: 行星必须离它很远才能维持生命。

imitations.en: An organism would have to adapt to extreme conditions to be capable of surviving.
imitations.zh: 有机体必须适应极端条件才能存活。

imitations.en: A telescope would have to be placed in space to be capable of detecting small objects.
imitations.zh: 望远镜必须放置在太空中才能探测到小物体。
